channel.title: Bavaria Medieval on Share Hub
channel.description: Recent content in Bavaria Medieval on Share Hub

item.title: Regensburg Travel Guide | UNESCO Medieval City 1.5 Hours from Munich (2026)
item.description:
About the Destination
#

Regensburg, nestled in the heart of Bavaria, Germany, is a city that time seems to have graciously overlooked. Located approximately 125 kilometers north of Munich, it stands proudly on the banks of the majestic Danube River. With a population of around 155,000 inhabitants, Regensburg is a vibrant yet remarkably tranquil city that boasts one of Europe’s most extensive and best-preserved medieval city centers. This historical jewel was rightfully recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2006, a testament to its enduring architectural legacy and cultural significance.

item.title: Füssen Travel Guide | Alpine Medieval Town Beyond Neuschwanstein (2026)
item.description: Everyone goes to Füssen for Neuschwanstein — but leaves without seeing the town itself. This guide is for the city: Hohes Schloss with its trompe l’œil windows, the Altstadt, the Lech River canyon and nearby Forggensee.

item.title: Memmingen Travel Guide | Medieval City 1.5 Hours from Munich (2026)
item.description: Memmingen is one of Bavaria’s least-known but best-preserved medieval towns. Near-complete city walls, Marktplatz, half-timbered buildings — a small but complete open-air museum. 1.5 hours from Munich with Deutschlandticket.
